name: API Contract Check
description: When a controller/endpoint file is created, verifies an API contract (OpenAPI spec) exists first. Enforces contract-before-implementation.

patterns: {DERIVE: controller file patterns from tech-stack.md + module-structure.md}

then.prompt:
Check .compliance-state.json → currentPhase. This hook applies from Foundation phase onward.

A controller/endpoint file was created. Verify API-First compliance:

1. API CONTRACT EXISTS: Check if an OpenAPI contract (or equivalent spec) exists for this module/endpoint. Look in contracts/ or the module's spec folder.
2. CONTRACT DEFINES THIS ENDPOINT: If a contract exists, verify it defines the endpoint being implemented.
3. CONTRACT REVIEWED: Ideally the contract was committed in a prior PR (not the same commit as the controller).

If no contract exists: WARN with rule GOV-API-001. Suggest creating the contract first.
If contract exists but doesn't define this endpoint: WARN.
If all rules pass, confirm compliance silently.

## Compliance Logging
After completing all checks, append to `compliance-log/events/{today-date}.jsonl`:
{"timestamp": "{ISO-8601-UTC}", "type": "check", "id": "chk-{date}-{time}-{seq}", "hook": "api-contract-check", "trigger": "fileCreated", "ruleId": "GOV-API-001", "ruleSeverity": "critical", "result": "{pass|fail|warn}", "message": "{finding}"}
